cityDNA.coreFantasy: A vast mesa-terrace capital where ancestral record, public training, terrace agriculture, and grain markets make sacred landscape into civic order.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Awanate is not a tribal village or lost ruin; it is a full Indigenous mesa civilization with residential courts, training arenas, archives, agricultural terraces, granaries, and sacred high-place authority.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Awanate turns memory, provision, discipline, and relationship to the Four Winds into a durable civic system expressed through carved totems, manor courts, training arenas, archive towers, terrace granaries, seed halls, and grain markets.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: White civic blocks, timber longhouses, reed pavilions, adobe walls, terrace stone, turquoise accents, red awnings, blue lamps, carved totems, training grounds, archive towers, granaries, garden terraces, and wind-facing platforms.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: An Indigenous American-coded capital where ancestral legitimacy, public training, archive memory, terrace farming, and food security are all civic responsibilities.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Carved totems, turquoise ancestor mesa, Four Winds, seed halls, champion gates, copyist halls, hanging gardens, grain halls, and awning markets symbolize memory carried forward through provision and discipline.

canonLocks: Awanate is an ancient Indigenous mesa-terrace civilization, not a generic tribal village or ruin.
The Wonder is the Turquoise Ancestor Mesa of the Four Winds.
The Beacon is the Manor Court Beacon.
The Seat of Power is the Carved Totems Sovereign Registry Court.
The five primary districts are High Residential, Arena / Training, Records / Archives, Agricultural Terraces, and Granary / Food Market.

The uploaded district anchors include High Residential Civic Hall, Training Arena, Archive Tower, Terrace Granary, Grain Hall, Hanging Gardens Monument, Champion Gate Nook, Copyist Hall Nook, Seed Hall Nook, Awning Monument, and Mill Court Nook.
provisionalCanon: Awanate's highest civic virtue is ancestral stewardship.
The city links family residence, training discipline, official records, seed continuity, and public food security into one civic ethic.
The repeated Hanging Gardens Monument suggests cultivated terrace beauty is a civic symbol, not only agricultural background.
rejectedReads: Awanate is primitive, temporary, or ruinous.
Awanate is only ceremonial and lacks civic infrastructure.
The mesa is background scenery rather than a civic ancestor anchor.
Food systems and records are secondary flavor rather than central city identity.
cautionFlags: Avoid pan-Indigenous flattening; keep the city specific to its authored mesa, carved totem, terrace, seed, and Four Winds civic language.
Do not make the city rustic; archives, courts, arenas, granaries, markets, and civic halls must remain visible.
Keep food, training, residential authority, and memory balanced.
Preserve exact sidecar anchors where possible instead of replacing them with generic equivalents.

environment.environmentalLogic: The mesa is a civic ancestor and organizing high ground. Farming, records, residence, training, and ceremony all orient around terraces, wind, seed, public continuity, and durable provision.

skyline: Turquoise Ancestor Mesa of the Four Winds as far wonder-scale anchor
Carved Totems Sovereign Registry Court as central authority
Manor Court Beacon as near/front signal landmark
archive towers, arena structures, granary halls, terrace fields, hanging gardens, grain halls, and longhouse roofs spread across the mesa city

civilization.people: Citizens are mesa-rooted, record-conscious, agriculturally literate, publicly disciplined, and oriented around ancestral responsibility rather than spectacle.
civilization.governance: Authority is ancestral-registry based, expressed through carved totems, public records, high residential legitimacy, food systems, training obligations, and ritual relationships to the mesa.
civilization.religion: Religious expression is integrated through ancestor mesa reverence, Four Winds symbolism, carved totems, public memory, seed rites, hanging gardens, and ceremonial relationships to land and sky.
customs: public respect for ancestors and record keepers
bodyLanguage: respectful orientation toward totems or mesa
careful handling of seed and record objects

streets.appearance: Broad mesa roads, terrace stairs, longhouse fronts, high residential courts, archive approaches, arena plazas, gardened slopes, grain market awnings, blue lamps, carved totem courts, and hanging garden overlooks.
streets.movement: Movement follows terraces, processional roads, manor courts, arena gates, archive stairs, seed halls, grain markets, mill courts, and wind-facing platforms; wind and elevation are constant orientation tools.
streets.lighting: Daylight is golden and open; night is blue-lamped around civic halls with warm lanterns in markets and residences.
streets.weatherEffects: Wind defines exposed terraces, banners, dust, and public ceremony; rain should make adobe, garden soil, and stone smell alive.
streets.dayCycle: Morning terrace work and market preparation; midday records, training, and public business; evening lanterns, family courts, and grain-market food.
streets.nightCycle: Blue lamps around registry, archive, and manor courts; quieter fields; warm longhouse and market lanterns under wind.

housing.wealthy: High residential courts, manor houses, carved timber, turquoise detail, gardened terraces, hanging garden views, and blue-lit courtyards near registry authority.
housing.middle: Longhouse courts, reed pavilions, adobe walls, shared gardens, seed storage, and terrace access.
housing.poor: Compact terrace dwellings, market-adjacent rooms, clay storage, shared water, woven shade, and practical but permanent civic materials.

districts.name: High Residential
districts.civicPurpose: Household continuity, local authority, public standing, ancestral residence, and Beacon-centered residential legitimacy.
districts.visualIdentity: High Residential Civic Hall, Manor Court Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Salon Pavilion Nook, manor courts, high terraces, longhouses, blue lamps, and carved totem approaches.

districts.name: Arena / Training
districts.civicPurpose: Public discipline, readiness, champion formation, embodied civic responsibility, and training-house legitimacy.
districts.visualIdentity: Arena / Training Civic Hall, Training Arena, Woven Banners Monument, Champion Gate Nook, lecture courts, obsidian stone, practice rings, and disciplined crowds.

districts.name: Records / Archives
districts.civicPurpose: Official memory, ancestry, civic record, legal continuity, and proof of belonging.
districts.visualIdentity: Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, white civic blocks, archive stairs, and quiet courts.

districts.name: Agricultural Terraces
districts.civicPurpose: Food production, seed continuity, field labor, land stewardship, and public proof that ancestral memory feeds the living.
districts.visualIdentity: Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook, field steps, irrigation channels, dryland crops, and garden stone.

districts.name: Granary / Food Market
districts.civicPurpose: Provisioning, milling, public food trust, market distribution, and grain accountability.
districts.visualIdentity: Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Grain Hall, Awning Monument, Mill Court Nook, covered market stalls, grain scales, storage jars, and red awnings.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Busy, nourishing, practical, and publicly accountable.

wonder.name: Turquoise Ancestor Mesa of the Four Winds
wonder.symbolicRole: Ancestral memory and sacred landscape made civic horizon.
wonder.visualRole: Far wonder-scale mesa anchor with turquoise identity, wind-facing platforms, terraces, ceremonial height, and visible continuity with the city's cultivated slopes.
wonder.civicRole: The deepest legitimacy marker for Awanate's relationship to memory, land, wind, food, and public continuity.
narrativeUse: judging a present dispute against ancestral stewardship
storyHooks: a record dispute must be judged against ancestral memory
a wind rite changes public understanding of a crisis
a seed continuity ceremony exposes a forgotten obligation

beacon.name: Manor Court Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public warning rooted in household and high residential legitimacy.
beacon.visualRole: Beacon rising from manor courts and high terraces with blue lamp identity, carved totem approaches, and cultivated high residential space around it.
beacon.civicRole: Signal heart for residential authority, public warning, and citywide coordination.
storyHooks: a warning splits household duty from public duty
the Beacon crowd reveals which families hold real standing

seatOfPower.name: Carved Totems Sovereign Registry Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Ancestral authority, civic record, and legal standing carved into public form.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Central registry terraces with carved totems, formal courts, white stone, turquoise accents, and blue civic lamps.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Government center for public record, ancestry, civic identity, legal continuity, seed claims, and household legitimacy.
narrativeUse: record-backed decisions about food or training duty
assetUse: visual source for carved totem motifs
storyHooks: a carved record contradicts a living household claim
a registry ruling changes who controls a seed hall

majorLandmarks.name: Training Arena
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Discipline and readiness embodied in public skill.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Arena ring, champion gate, woven banners, lecture court, practice grounds, and disciplined spectators.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Forms champions, teaches readiness, and lets the community witness competence.
storyHooks: a public test exposes hidden unreadiness
a champion gate ceremony becomes political

majorLandmarks.name: Archive Tower
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Memory raised above the city and guarded from forgetfulness.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Tall archive structure with copyist hall, gold-seal monument, quiet stairs, and white civic massing.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Preserves ancestry, law, record, and public continuity.
storyHooks: a copyist hall record proves a forgotten obligation
a gold-seal document changes a food dispute

majorLandmarks.name: Terrace Granary
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Granary on agricultural terraces with seed hall, hanging gardens, irrigation channels, and field steps.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Anchors food production, seed continuity, terrace stewardship, and seasonal resilience.
storyHooks: a seed hall claim determines who eats next season
a damaged terrace becomes a civic emergency

majorLandmarks.name: Grain Hall
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Food trust made public, measured, and accountable.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Covered food-market hall with Awning Monument, Mill Court, grain scales, storage jars, red awnings, and busy public stalls.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Manages distribution, milling, grain weighing, market provisioning, and public confidence in food systems.
storyHooks: a grain measure is correct but politically explosive
the Mill Court reveals whether scarcity is real or manufactured

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show High Residential Civic Hall, Manor Court Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Salon Pavilion Nook, manor courts, totems, high terraces, and blue lamps.
S2 must center Arena / Training Civic Hall, Training Arena, Woven Banners Monument, Champion Gate Nook, practice rings, lecture court, trainers, and spectators.
S3 must show Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, archive stairs, clerks, and sealed records.
S4 must show Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook, field steps, irrigation, seed storage, and terrace farmers.
S5 must show Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Grain Hall, Awning Monument, Mill Court Nook, red awnings, grain scales, storage jars, and market crowds.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Use a mesa-terrace city with white civic blocks, timber longhouses, reed pavilions, adobe, carved totems, turquoise accents, red awnings, blue lamps, archive towers, training arenas, granaries, garden terraces, and wind-facing platforms.

The Turquoise Ancestor Mesa of the Four Winds must remain the far wonder-scale anchor; the Carved Totems Sovereign Registry Court should be central authority; the Manor Court Beacon should mark high residential signal identity.
Show food systems and records as civic infrastructure, not background agriculture.
requiredVisualElements: Turquoise Ancestor Mesa of the Four Winds
elementsToAvoid: empty ceremonial mesa with no civic infrastructure
food systems treated as rural background
training or records omitted from city identity
pure fantasy palace architecture without terrace logic
